title: Dream Rejection (candidate move that failed the gate)
description: One rejected candidate from the moves substrate's eval-gated promotion loop (dream Phase 6b). Written by `run_promotion_pass` to `~/.trinity/dream_rejections.jsonl` (one row per line). Captures which tier of the four-tier Bayesian gate (T1 lexical → T2 embedding → T3 chairman → T4 posterior) rejected the candidate and why — the introspection surface for debugging the gate's behavior. The rejection log is append-only; nothing in Trinity reads it for routing decisions, only for human inspection + analytics.

candidate_name.description: The proposed Move's `name` field (the human-readable title from SKILL.md frontmatter). Not slugified — the rejection log preserves the original prose so users can grep for what dream tried to surface.

candidate_basin.description: The basin (topic-cluster id from `topics.json`) the candidate would have served. Null when the candidate wasn't basin-anchored — rare; discovery groups rejections by basin, so most candidates carry one.

rejected_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp (seconds precision) when the gate rejected this candidate. Matches the convention used elsewhere in `~/.trinity/`.

why_rejected.description: Short human-readable rationale composed from the failing tier's reason field. Stored separately from `tier_results` so a one-line summary is available without parsing the per-tier breakdown.

promoted_from.description: The rejection-signal ids (from `~/.trinity/me/rejections.jsonl`) that seeded this candidate. Lets users trace a rejected candidate back to the user_substitute exemplars it was synthesized from — useful when the gate consistently rejects candidates from a particular basin (signals a basin where the user's pattern isn't yet captured).

tier_results.description: Per-tier breakdown — every tier the candidate reached before rejection, in evaluation order. Always at least one entry (the failing tier); may include earlier-tier passes when the candidate cleared T1+T2 but failed T3. T4 only fires on demotion, never on candidate evaluation.

tier.description: Which gate tier produced this result. T1 = lexical Jaccard vs accepted patterns in the same basin. T2 = embedding cosine vs basin centroid. T3 = chairman eval against the basin's rejection corpus. T4 = Beta-Binomial posterior; demotion-only.

passed.description: Whether the tier accepted the candidate. The failing tier is the last entry with `passed: false`; subsequent tiers don't run.

score.description: Tier-specific score (0..1). T1: Jaccard fraction. T2: cosine. T3: aggregate axis-score from the chairman eval rubric. T4: posterior. Null when the tier was skipped (cold install).

threshold.description: The minimum score the candidate had to clear at this tier. T1 default: 0.3. T2 default: 0.7. T3 default: 0.5 fallback or trinity_eval_baseline when re-eval. Null on vacuous-pass tiers.

reason.description: One-line explanation of the pass/fail decision in human language. Lifted into `why_rejected` at the top level when this is the failing tier.
